basic_info.title: Parasite Eve
basic_info.review: Parasite Eve is a groundbreaking cinematic RPG that blends survival horror with JRPG elements, featuring innovative real-time combat and a gripping sci-fi story about mitochondria and evolution.

alternative_names: パラサイト・イヴ (Parasaito Ivu)

description.synopsis: A young NYPD officer named Aya Brea must stop a mysterious entity called Eve from destroying humanity through spontaneous human combustion and mitochondrial evolution.
key_features: Pausable real-time combat system with ATB mechanics
Cinematic RPG style blending survival horror and JRPG elements
New Game+ mode (EX game) with additional content
Innovative Parasite Energy (PE) system for attacks and defense
Pre-rendered 3D backgrounds with real-world New York locations
description.long_description: Parasite Eve, developed by SquareSoft in 1998, is a groundbreaking cinematic RPG that blends survival horror with traditional JRPG elements. Directed by Takashi Tokita and produced by Hironobu Sakaguchi (Final Fantasy), the game follows NYPD officer Aya Brea as she investigates mysterious spontaneous human combustion incidents in New York City. The story centers on Eve, a woman whose mitochondria have evolved to create an 'Ultimate Being' that threatens humanity's existence. The game features SquareSoft's first M-rated content and was their first major collaboration between Japanese and American development teams. With music composed by Yoko Shimomura (Kingdom Hearts), the soundtrack incorporates opera and electronica influences, featuring the vocal track 'Somnia Memorias.' The combat system uses a unique pausable real-time ATB mechanic where players can dodge attacks during turns. Upon completion, an EX Game mode unlocks with additional content including the Chrysler Building dungeon. The game received generally favorable reviews (81/100 on Metacritic) and has been recognized as one of the greatest RPGs of all time, influencing countless horror RPGs that followed.
legacy_and_impact: Pioneered the cinematic RPG genre blending survival horror with JRPG mechanics
First SquareSoft game rated M by ESRB
Established Yoko Shimomura as a major video game composer
Influenced numerous horror RPGs including Shadow Hearts and Koudelka
Part of the J-horror gaming phenomenon alongside Ring and The Grudge
Spawned two sequels: Parasite Eve II (1999) and The 3rd Birthday (2010)
Featured in PlayStation Classic lineup as a flagship title
